nodes.label: AISStream WebSocket
nodes.description: Real-time AIS vessel position data via WebSocket. Free tier provides global coverage. We connect for 5-minute hourly snapshots within 200nm bounding boxes around our target ports.

nodes.label: NOAA Marine API
nodes.description: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. Provides marine weather observations: wave height, wind speed, swell period, and storm alerts. Free, no API key required.

nodes.label: NOAA Tides & Currents
nodes.description: NOAA CO-OPS API for tide predictions and water levels at port stations. Critical for berth availability — large vessels cannot dock during low tides.

nodes.label: CMEMS Ocean Currents
nodes.description: Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service. Provides ocean surface current vectors and sea surface temperature. Currents affect vessel speed and therefore ETA into port.

nodes.label: FRED Economics API
nodes.description: Federal Reserve Economic Data. We ingest GSCPI (supply chain pressure), DCOILWTICO (crude oil price), and DTWEXBGS (trade-weighted dollar index). These macro signals correlate with port volumes weeks ahead.

nodes.label: GDELT News Feed
nodes.description: Global Database of Events, Language, and Tone. We filter for maritime disruption keywords (strike, blockade, grounding). News events can predict sudden congestion spikes 12-24h before AIS data reflects them.

nodes.label: UN Comtrade API
nodes.description: United Nations trade flow database. Monthly import/export volumes by commodity and country. Seasonal trade surges (Q4 holiday shipping) correlate with port congestion 2-4 weeks later.

nodes.label: Port Loader
nodes.description: Loads target port coordinates and generates 200nm (3.3°) bounding boxes for AIS filtering. Reads from port configuration and produces the geographic boundaries that tell the AIS client which area of the ocean to monitor.

nodes.label: AIS Stream Client
nodes.description: Async WebSocket client that connects to AISStream, receives vessel position messages, validates them with Pydantic models, buffers into batches, and flushes to S3 as NDJSON. Supports max_duration_seconds for the 5-minute hourly batch mode.

nodes.label: Weather Client
nodes.description: Fetches marine weather observations from Open-Meteo Marine API for all target port coordinates. Returns wave height, swell period, wind speed, and sea surface temperature. Validates output with Pandera schema before S3 upload.

nodes.label: NOAA Tides Client
nodes.description: Fetches tide predictions and water level observations from NOAA CO-OPS stations near target ports. Water levels affect berth accessibility for deep-draft vessels.

nodes.label: CMEMS Client
nodes.description: Downloads ocean current data from Copernicus Marine Service using the copernicusmarine Python API. Extracts surface current speed and direction within port approach areas.

nodes.label: Carrier Scrapers
nodes.description: Web scrapers for container shipping lines (Maersk, CMA CGM, MSC). Extract vessel schedules, port calls, and delay announcements. Built on a shared BaseCarrierClient with rate limiting and retry logic.

nodes.label: Settings & Config
nodes.description: Pydantic BaseSettings class that loads all API keys, S3 bucket names, database credentials, and port configurations from environment variables (.env file). Single source of truth for all secrets.

nodes.label: S3 Writer
nodes.description: Utility module that writes NDJSON batches to S3 with Hive-style partitioning (source=ais/date=2026-05-04/). Handles boto3 client creation, content encoding, and upload error handling.

nodes.label: S3 Raw Bucket
nodes.description: AWS S3 bucket storing raw NDJSON files partitioned by source and date. This is the immutable data lake layer — raw data is never modified, only appended. Structure: s3://dpl-raw/raw/source={name}/date={YYYY-MM-DD}/{timestamp}.ndjson

nodes.label: Hourly Ingestion Flow
nodes.description: Prefect @flow that submits all ingestion tasks concurrently using .submit(). Runs on cron '0 * * * *' (top of every hour). Each task has retries=3 with exponential backoff [10s, 30s, 60s]. AIS task runs for exactly 5 minutes then shuts down gracefully.

nodes.label: AIS Streaming Flow
nodes.description: Dedicated Prefect @flow for the async AIS WebSocket consumer. Originally designed for 24/7 streaming, now being refactored into a 5-minute batch task within the hourly ingestion flow.

nodes.label: Hourly dbt Flow
nodes.description: Prefect @flow that runs at '15 * * * *' (15 minutes past each hour). Three sequential tasks: (1) load NDJSON from S3 → Postgres raw tables, (2) run dbt build (models + tests), (3) check source freshness. Offset by 15 minutes to give ingestion flow time to finish.

nodes.label: Postgres raw.* Tables
nodes.description: Raw data loaded from S3 NDJSON into Postgres tables: raw_ais_positions, raw_weather_observations, raw_fred_indicators, raw_comtrade_flows, raw_gdelt_events. These are the dbt 'sources' — the starting point for all transformations.

nodes.label: stg_ais_positions
nodes.description: Staging view that cleans raw AIS data: casts types, deduplicates by (mmsi, timestamp), and classifies vessel zone (at_berth, at_anchor, approaching, in_transit) based on speed and distance from port center using a Haversine approximation.

nodes.label: stg_weather_observations
nodes.description: Staging view that cleans weather data: casts numeric columns, filters impossible values (wave_height > 25m), and standardizes timestamps to UTC.

nodes.label: stg_comtrade_flows
nodes.description: Staging view that cleans UN Comtrade trade flow data: standardizes country codes, filters to maritime-relevant HS commodity codes, and casts monetary values.

nodes.label: stg_fred_indicators
nodes.description: Staging view that cleans FRED economic data: converts FRED's '.' missing value sentinel to NULL, casts dates, and filters to only the 3 tracked series (GSCPI, DCOILWTICO, DTWEXBGS).

nodes.label: mart_port_congestion
nodes.description: Mart table that calculates the daily congestion score per port: total_vessels, vessels_at_berth, vessels_at_anchor, vessels_approaching, and congestion_score (ratio of anchored to total). Being refactored to hourly grain for the new system.

nodes.label: mart_vessel_daily
nodes.description: Mart table that aggregates per-vessel daily statistics: avg speed, max speed, speed variability, total distance traveled, time spent at anchor vs. in transit. These behavioral features feed the congestion model.

nodes.label: mart_port_status_hourly
nodes.description: NEW mart table for the pivot. Joins hourly vessel counts (per 10nm/50nm/200nm ring), weather data, and macro indicators into a single row per port per hour. This is the primary feature table for the LightGBM congestion classifier.

nodes.label: mart_vessel_inbound
nodes.description: NEW mart table. Aggregates inbound vessel counts into range rings (10nm, 50nm, 200nm) per port per hour. Uses Haversine distance approximation. This is the strongest predictive signal for future congestion.

nodes.label: dbt Data Quality Tests
nodes.description: 8 custom SQL tests validating data quality: coordinate ranges (-90/90), speed sanity (<50 kts), congestion score [0,1], no future timestamps, FRED series completeness, weather coverage per port, no duplicate GDELT events, positive trade values.

nodes.label: Feast Feature Repo
nodes.description: Feast feature store definitions. Registers feature views for port hourly status, inbound vessel counts, weather, and macro indicators. Provides point-in-time correct joins for training to prevent data leakage in time-series forecasting.

nodes.label: Feast Online Store
nodes.description: Feast online store (backed by Postgres or Redis). Stores the latest feature values for real-time inference. The FastAPI service queries this to get current port state when making predictions.

nodes.label: Congestion Target
nodes.description: Binary classification target: is_congested_24h. Defined as 1 when the future (T+24h) anchorage count exceeds the port's 75th percentile historical threshold. Computed via a self-join in dbt on the hourly anchorage counts table.

nodes.label: LightGBM Classifier
nodes.description: Gradient-boosted tree classifier trained to predict is_congested_24h from hourly port features. Chosen over deep learning for interpretability: tree-based SHAP values provide exact feature importance that powers the dashboard's transparency panel.

nodes.label: MLflow Model Registry
nodes.description: MLflow tracking server for experiment logging and model versioning. Each training run logs hyperparameters, F1/ROC-AUC metrics, SHAP plots, and the serialized model artifact. The production model is promoted via the Registry's staging → production workflow.

nodes.label: SHAP Feature Importance
nodes.description: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values computed on each prediction. Tells us exactly how much each feature contributed: '45% inbound vessels at 50nm, 30% wave height, 15% anchorage count'. This is serialized and sent to the dashboard transparency panel.

nodes.label: FastAPI Inference Service
nodes.description: Python REST API that serves congestion predictions. Endpoints: GET /api/ports/{code}/status (current metrics), GET /api/ports/{code}/forecast (48h congestion predictions), GET /api/ports/{code}/vessels (inbound vessel list), GET /api/ports/{code}/sources (data source health).

nodes.label: AWS ECS Fargate
nodes.description: Serverless container runtime. Runs both the hourly batch ingestion tasks and the always-on FastAPI service. No EC2 instances to manage. Estimated cost: $2-3/month for the batch tasks, ~$5/month for the API service (0.25 vCPU, 0.5 GB).

nodes.label: Port Intelligence Dashboard
nodes.description: Interactive single-page HTML/CSS/JS dashboard. Features: Leaflet map with 10/50/200nm range rings, color-coded vessel markers, port selector (5 ports), congestion forecast chart (1-48h), inbound vessel table with ETA & confidence, 6-week trend line, and a data source transparency panel showing feature contribution percentages.
